title: Hieracium tanylobum
intro: Hieracium tanylobum, also known as the hawkweed or tanylobum hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium tanylobum include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as triterpenes and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium tanylobum include tinctures, teas, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
uses: Hieracium tanylobum is not a widely studied plant, and as a result, there is limited information available on its medicinal uses.
